title: delivery.rs circuit breaker eviction retains all entries with failures>0
description: delivery.rs circuit_record_failure() (line 81): the first eviction pass retains entries where 'consecutive_failures > 0'. Since this function only adds/increments entries, all entries in the map have consecutive_failures > 0. This means the first retain() is a no-op and only the second pass (keeping only open circuits) actually evicts. The intent appears to be 'retain entries that still matter' but the condition is always true. The first retain should use a staleness heuristic like 'last activity within N hours' instead.
